Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: A high strength Al–Li alloy produced by laser powder bed fusion: Densification, microstructure, and mechanical properties

Composition_Info.Note: Li loss of ~24% during LPBF processing; composition still meets AA2195 standard. Measured on LPBF sample fabricated with optimized parameters.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized Al-Cu-Li-Mg-Ag-Zr powders (mean particle size 35.3 μm) processed by LPBF under protective argon atmosphere. Optimized parameters: laser power 200 W, layer thickness 30 μm, scanning velocity 80 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.1 mm, hatch rotation 90° between consecutive layers. Sample built at room temperature. Tensile plates built perpendicular to building direction.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF of gas-atomized 2195 powder with optimized parameters: 200 W, 30 μm layer thickness, 80 mm/s scanning speed, 0.1 mm hatch spacing.

Process_Info.Equipment: Self-developed LPBF machine (Huazhong University of Science and Technology)

Microstructure_Text.original: Coarse columnar grain microstructure with epitaxial growth through multiple building layers. MP boundaries and columnar grain boundaries clearly distinguishable from MP interiors. MP irregularity responsible for remaining unfused defects. Low thermal conductivity of 2195 leads to low solidification rate, resulting in wider and longer columnarity. Low Zr content (<0.16%) cannot provide enough Al3Zr nuclei for heterogeneous nucleation. Three precipitate forms observed: white precipitates rich in Cu and Mg (round or elongated), grey precipitates rich in O (Al2O3), black precipitates with low Al content indicating Li-containing phase δ' (Al3Li). Heterogeneous element distribution with Cu and Ag enrichment at grain and MP boundaries. High density of dislocations.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Coarse columnar grains with epitaxial growth; heterogeneous microstructure with Cu/Mg-rich precipitates at boundaries, Al2O3 oxide particles, and δ' (Al3Li) phase; inhomogeneous element distribution; high dislocation density.

Composition_Info.Note: Same LPBF-processed composition as as-fabricated sample, with same Li loss (~24%). After T6 treatment, elements dissolved into Al matrix and uniformly precipitated.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized Al-Cu-Li-Mg-Ag-Zr powders (mean particle size 35.3 μm) processed by LPBF under protective argon atmosphere. Optimized parameters: laser power 200 W, layer thickness 30 μm, scanning velocity 80 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.1 mm, hatch rotation 90° between consecutive layers. Then T6 heat treatment: solutionized at 520°C for 1 h, water quenched, aged at 170°C for 6 h, air cooled to room temperature. Tensile plates built perpendicular to building direction.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF of gas-atomized 2195 powder with optimized parameters (200 W, 30 μm, 80 mm/s, 0.1 mm hatch), followed by T6 heat treatment (520°C/1h solution + 170°C/6h aging).

Process_Info.Equipment: Self-developed LPBF machine (Huazhong University of Science and Technology)

Microstructure_Text.original: Coarse columnar grain microstructure similar to as-fabricated sample, indicating T6 treatment did not change columnarity character. Homogeneous microstructure after T6 treatment with uniform Mg and Ag distribution. Cu-containing particles evenly distributed. T1 (Al2CuLi) plates precipitate in areas with high dislocation density. T1 phase also precipitates at locations with low dislocation density. T1 plates effectively impede dislocation movement. High density of dislocations retained after T6 treatment.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Coarse columnar grains retained; homogeneous element distribution after T6; dominant T1 (Al2CuLi) plate precipitates at dislocations; high dislocation density retained.

Properties_Info.Note: Based on LAGBs (0-5°) KAM analysis; lower than as-fabricated (2.13×10^13 /m^2) but still high

Composition_Info.Note: Gas-atomized powder starting material. Mean particle size 35.3 μm. Standard composition range for AA2195: Cu 3.7-4.3, Li 0.8-1.2, Mg 0.25-0.8, Ag 0.25-0.6, Zr 0.08-0.16, Al Bal.

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized Al-Cu-Li-Mg-Ag-Zr powders supplied by Intelligence Manufacturing Technology. Powder particles exhibit nearly spherical shape with mean particle size of 35.3 μm.
Process_Text.simplified: Gas-atomized 2195 Al-Li alloy powder, spherical morphology, 35.3 μm mean particle size.

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial AA2195-T6 plate reference from literature (Kim et al., 2016). Requires 180 h aging duration. Properties compared in Table 3.

Process_Text.original: Commercial AA2195 plate subjected to T6 heat treatment (solutionized, quenched, aged). Requires up to 180 h aging duration per standard practice. Data from Kim et al. (2016).
Process_Text.simplified: Commercial AA2195-T6 plate with 180 h aging treatment.

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial AA2195-T8 plate reference from literature (Kim et al., 2016). Requires 2.5% deformation before aging and 20 h aging duration. LPBF-T6 achieves similar strength properties.

Process_Text.original: Commercial AA2195 plate subjected to T8 heat treatment (solutionized, quenched, 2.5% deformation, aged 20 h). Data from Kim et al. (2016). Work hardening combined with precipitation strengthening.
Process_Text.simplified: Commercial AA2195-T8 plate with 2.5% pre-stretch deformation and 20 h aging.
